title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Andover police unions demand Chief Charles Gray's removal following Kelsey Fitzsimmons's acquittal and a no confidence vote.
Unions cite leadership failures such as lack of body-worn cameras and not addressing officer-involved shooting policies.
Chief Gray has led since 2016 and faced previous concerns in a 2020 report that he allegedly ignored, unions claim.

title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics are reportedly interested in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo as the Bucks face challenges in the Eastern Conference.
Giannis Antetokounmpo remains under contract with the Milwaukee Bucks through 2028, with a $58.4 million salary for next season.
Antetokounmpo could become a free-agent after next season due to a player option, impacting potential trade strategies for the Celtics.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox beat the Brewers 5-0, earning their first series win of the season.
Sonny Gray pitched 6 1/3 scoreless innings, helping the Red Sox stabilize with improved performances.
Tyler Samaniego made his MLB debut, striking out the side in a scoreless eighth inning.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget for fiscal year 2027 with a 2.1% spending increase.
The city faces financial challenges due to rising costs, inflation, and a $48.4 million budget deficit.
Wu's school budget proposal includes a $1.7 billion allocation with layoffs to adjust for decreasing enrollment.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are finalists for the Patriots Hall of Fame ballot this year.
Rob Gronkowski is a five-time Pro Bowler who retired as a Patriot and holds multiple team touchdowns and yards records.
Adam Vinatieri, a Pro Football Hall of Fame electee, holds NFL records for points and field goals, playing ten years with the Patriots.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins sign 19-year-old prospect James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract for the 2025-26 NHL season.
Hagens, from Boston College, joins Bruins after six games with Providence Bruins, contributing one goal and three assists.
Hagens could impact the Bruins' depth and power play with his high-end skating and playmaking skills during the playoffs.

title: Sen. Markey, local clergy call for end to Iran war and Trump’s time in office
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and Boston clergy call for an end to U.S. involvement in the Iran war at a Boston Common event.
Markey condemns President Trump's rhetoric and urges Congress to remove him, labeling Trump unfit to hold nuclear codes.
Mass Peace Action members and faith leaders criticize Trump's actions and call for assertive congressional measures to end the conflict.

title: Readers say their electric bills are ‘outrageously high.’ Here’s how much they pay.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A poll revealed 200 out of 239 Boston.com readers find their electric bills unmanageable due to rising costs.
Many respondents cite high delivery fees and sharp price increases as reasons for their escalating electric bills.
Residents served by municipal utilities report lower electricity costs compared to those with investor-owned providers.

title: A Nordic-inspired sauna just opened in the White Mountains
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: White Mountain Sauna Haus opens in North Conway, N.H., featuring Nordic-inspired décor with Finnish saunas and plunge pools.
The communal space includes a cafe, fire pit, and deck with scenic views, and promotes relaxation and social interaction.
Open to visitors 12 and older, the sauna does not take reservations; single visit costs $36, five visits $154, ten visits $286.

title: What Patriots coach Mike Vrabel said about viral photos with NFL reporter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel and NFL insider Dianna Russini defended photos of them at an Arizona resort as innocent.
The photos, published by the New York Post, showed Vrabel and Russini holding hands and sharing a hug at a Sedona resort.
Russini stated the photos excluded the larger group they were with, supported by The Athletic's executive editor's comments.
